skill.name: url-citation-search
skill.description: Find papers and preprints that cite a given URL, especially blogs, docs pages, project pages, or other web content that standard citation indexes often miss.
skill.summary: Reverse-search academic citations for web URLs with verification and deduplication.
skill.author: zjsxply

security_audit.summary: All 27 static findings are false positives. The external_commands flags match Markdown backticks used for inline code formatting in documentation text, not executable shell or Ruby commands. The weak-cryptography flags match harmless words inside the YAML frontmatter description field. The file contains only instructional documentation with no executable code, network calls, file system access, or environment access.

content.user_title: Find Academic Papers That Cite Any URL
content.value_statement: Standard citation indexes often miss blog posts, documentation pages, and project pages. This skill reverse-searches the academic web to find confirmed papers and preprints that cite a specific URL, with full verification and deduplication.
actual_capabilities: Resolves target pages and extracts canonical URLs, titles, and variants
Searches general web and academic sources for citations to a given URL
Verifies citations by inspecting PDFs, HTML reference lists, and metadata
Deduplicates preprints and published versions with separate fallback links
Distinguishes direct citations, official variants, and mirror citations
Outputs brief lists, detailed citation reports, or BibTeX entries
limitations: Requires live web access to fetch pages and perform searches
PDF text extraction can be lossy and may miss some link annotations
Sparse or newly published content may have no discoverable citations yet
Verification depends on the quality of publisher HTML and open-access PDFs

use_cases.title: Track impact of a blog post or project page
use_cases.description: Researchers and open-source maintainers can discover which papers have cited their non-traditional web content.

use_cases.title: Build a literature review from a seed URL
use_cases.description: Students and reviewers can start from a key resource and find all academic works that reference it.

use_cases.title: Verify citation claims in manuscripts
use_cases.description: Editors and authors can confirm whether a specific web URL is actually cited in a given paper or preprint.

prompt_templates.title: Basic URL citation search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all papers that cite this URL: https://example.com/blog-post. Return a brief list with title, year, and link.

prompt_templates.title: Detailed citation report
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for papers citing https://example.com/project-page. Provide a full citation report with DOI or arXiv ID, evidence notes, and whether each work is published or a preprint.

prompt_templates.title: BibTeX export
prompt_templates.prompt: Find papers that cite https://example.com/docs. Return BibTeX entries for the citing papers. Deduplicate preprints and published versions.

prompt_templates.title: Mirror and variant tracking
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for citations to https://example.com/page. Check for official URL variants, moved domains, and mirrored content. Distinguish direct citations from mirror citations in the report.

output: 1. Smith et al. (2023) – 'Neural Networks for Tabular Data' – https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.12345 (preprint)
2. Doe & Lee (2024) – 'Advanced Feature Engineering' – https://doi.org/10.1000/xyz789 (published) – cites official variant /research/blog-post

output_examples.input: Get BibTeX for papers citing https://example.com/project-page

best_practices: Always verify citations by inspecting the actual PDF or HTML reference list, not just search snippets
Check for official URL variants and moved domains before concluding no citations exist
Deduplicate preprints and published versions to keep reports clean and accurate
anti_patterns: Assuming a paper on the same topic is a citation without checking the reference list
Relying only on exact-string full-text search when general web search may recover more results
Returning search snippets as proof of citation without opening the source document

faq.question: What kinds of URLs can this skill search for?
faq.answer: It works best with blog posts, documentation pages, project pages, demos, and GitHub repositories. Standard papers with DOIs are usually better tracked through traditional citation indexes.

faq.question: How does the skill verify that a paper actually cites the URL?
faq.answer: It inspects the PDF text, HTML reference list, or publisher metadata to confirm the target URL, title, slug, or a verified mirror URL appears in the bibliography.

faq.question: What is the difference between a direct citation and a mirror citation?
faq.answer: A direct citation references the exact URL you provided. A mirror citation references a copy of the same content hosted on a different domain or path.

faq.question: Why might arXiv full-text search miss some citations?
faq.answer: arXiv full-text search can miss quoted strings or URL variants. The skill falls back to general web search and batch inspection of candidate papers to recover those citations.

faq.question: Can I get BibTeX for the citing papers?
faq.answer: Yes. Ask for BibTeX output and the skill will return entries for the citing papers. It will only return BibTeX for the target URL if you explicitly request it.

faq.question: What happens if the same work exists as both a preprint and a published paper?
faq.answer: The skill prefers the published version in the main list and keeps the preprint as an access fallback. It verifies each version separately because citations may differ between them.
